subject: Audio quality degradation after firmware upgrade
symptom: Intermittent audio drops on all consoles after overnight firmware upgrade
assessment: Root cause was OPUS codec sample-rate mismatch introduced in firmware v4.1.2. APEX defaulted to 48 kHz but the updated gateway expected 16 kHz, causing fallback to G.711 and brief RTP re-negotiation gaps.
resolution: Carbyne engineering pushed a corrective codec profile config. Vendor confirmed the regression. Forked A (Engineering Jira REP-8812).

subject: Intermittent dead air on 911 calls after APEX update
symptom: Callers hearing 2-4 seconds of silence before audio reconnects; issue site-wide
assessment: SRTP key exchange timing regression in APEX 5.2.0. Logs showed renegotiation events on every call open. Not a network issue — Datadog showed zero packet loss at the site level.
resolution: Hotfix applied by engineering (REP-9001). Forked A.
